title: Trump Seeks to Abolish Iran’s Nuclear Stockpile, a Problem He Helped Create
summary: President Trump withdrew from the Obama-era nuclear accord in 2018, saying it was the worst deal ever. But Iran responded with an enrichment spree that haunts the negotiations to this day.

title: On Iran’s Border, Cooking Oil Trade Is a Snapshot of the Country’s Struggles
summary: The war has exacerbated Iran’s economic crisis, forcing many to cross the border into Turkey to buy the most basic goods.

title: Firm Building Trump’s Ballroom Got a Secret No-Bid Contract for a Nearby Job
summary: The National Park Service increased the value of the contract several times over and then awarded it to Maryland-based Clark Construction, in a process that experts said was highly unusual.

title: A Year Later, Trump’s ‘Most Exclusive’ Memecoin Event Is a Lot Less Exclusive
summary: Even a Times reporter qualified for the event, which caused outrage last year for providing access to President Trump in exchange for investment in one of his family’s crypto ventures.

title: Texas Can Arrest and Deport People Who Illegally Cross at Mexico Border, Court Says
summary: The court that paused a 2023 law allowing state and local police officers to arrest migrants has now ruled that the measure is legal, a decision likely to be appealed.

title: In Deep Blue California, a G.O.P.-Backed Voter ID Proposal Makes the Ballot
summary: A Republican-backed initiative has cleared the signature threshold for the November election. Critics say the measure could make it harder for people to vote.

title: Every Black Republican Is Leaving the House, Erasing Diversity Gains
summary: All four Black House Republicans are retiring after this year, a reflection of the striking and persistent lack of diversity in the G.O.P. ranks of Congress.

title: Why Has ‘Christ Is King’ Become a Controversial Statement?
summary: For some, the three words have become encoded with a message of hate.

title: Democrats Weigh Whether a Lawmaker’s Ethnicity Counts More Than Ideology
summary: The same progressive South Asian networks that helped elect Zohran Mamdani as mayor in New York are mobilizing against Jenifer Rajkumar, a Queens assemblywoman.

title: New Taxes Helped Cool London’s Housing Market. Could That Happen in New York?
summary: Economists and real estate agents are calling London’s taxation of wealthy property owners a cautionary tale for New York, where leaders have endorsed a second-home tax.

title: Trump, Iran’s Newest Hostage
summary: The president is buried in the graveyard of vanity.

title: The Town That Reveals All of Trump’s Bad Economic Ideas
summary: The jobs are coming back, despite President Trump’s tariffs and harsh immigration enforcement.

title: 2 C.I.A. Officers Killed in Mexico Crash Lacked Proper Authorization
summary: The two Americans were killed on Sunday when their vehicle crashed while returning from an antidrug operation led by Mexico’s armed forces in the state of Chihuahua.

title: Al Qaeda-Linked Militants Launch Major Attacks on Cities Across Mali
summary: The armed group JNIM claimed to have seized two key cities and destroyed the defense minister’s residence in a coordinated offensive that experts said was a major escalation in yearslong hostilities.

title: ‘Michael’ is Expected to Collect $200 Million in First Weekend, Shaking Off Bad Reviews
summary: Lionsgate estimated on Saturday that the Michael Jackson biopic would collect more than $200 million over its first few days in theaters.
